get.summary: Get List of Call Log Files
get.description:
Phone.com created the async-call-logs API endpoint to allow customers to download large amounts of call logs without encountering the normal Phone.com API Rate Limits. 
This service needs to be activated on a per-customer basis and collect the call log data in the requested customer's format. (please call our support to activate and better define the format if needed) 
When the async-call-logs endpoint is invoked, the endpoint will return a list of 47 to 48 files, each representing the calls entered into the logs during a full hour of the last two days. The file names contain the time period of call logs each file encapsulates. 
The Items returned are ordered by modification (i.e., creation) date; the most recent item is the first item. The files should be download using the provided URL(s) in the item JSON array returned by the endpoint. 
Phone.com creates the files at the 15 minutes mark, every hour for the previous hour. Files lifespan is 2 days, which means that each file will disappear after 48 hours.

This endpoint provides access to asynchronously generated files with call logs.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return

parameters.name: sort[id]
parameters.description: Sort response

parameters.name: include_subaccounts
parameters.description: Send "1" if you want to add subaccounts data into output

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Defines mode of the data output (brief or full)

get.description:
The Call Logs API returns a list of call detail records for the account. The result includes the id, start time, creation time, caller ID, called number, direction of call, and duration in seconds. If applicable, the result may also include final action, final action duration, listen URL, and/or transcription. Since the call logs usually contains many calls, API users may use filters such as start_time, created_at and direction to limit the result set to calls interested. Users may also use the sorting parameters such as start_time and created_at to list result in ascending or descending order.

Get a list of call details associated with your account

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return

parameters.name: sort[id]
parameters.description: Sort response

parameters.name: include_subaccounts
parameters.description: Send "1" if you want to add subaccounts data into output

parameters.name: filters[id]
parameters.description: Call UUID

parameters.name: filters[start_time]
parameters.description: Call start time filter. Expected value is UNIX timestamp with operator like "gt" (greater than), "gte" (greater than or equals), "lt" (lesser than), "lte" (lesser than or equals). Example: "gt:1597778180".

parameters.name: filters[created_at]
parameters.description: Call log creation time filter. Expected value is UNIX timestamp with operator like "gt" (greater than), "gte" (greater than or equals), "lt" (lesser than), "lte" (lesser than or equals). Example: "gt:1597778180".

parameters.name: filters[direction]
parameters.description: Call direction filter: in or out

parameters.name: filters[type]
parameters.description: Call type, such as 'call', 'fax', 'audiogram'

parameters.name: filters[extension]
parameters.description: Account extension filter

parameters.name: filters[called_number]
parameters.description: Called number

parameters.name: filters[caller]
parameters.description: Number of the caller

parameters.name: filters[number]
parameters.description: Filter by your number (no matter if it is inbound or outbound call). Accepts list of numbers: "in:%2B15555550001,%2B15555550002" ("%2B" stands for "+" in query string).

parameters.name: filters[other_number]
parameters.description: Filter by other party's number (no matter if it is inbound or outbound call). Accepts list of numbers: "in:%2B15555550001,%2B15555550002" ("%2B" stands for "+" in query string).

parameters.name: filters[call_duration]
parameters.description: Duration of the call

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Defines mode of the data output (brief or full)

get.description:

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return

parameters.name: sort[id]
parameters.description: Sort response

parameters.name: include_subaccounts
parameters.description: Send "1" if you want to add subaccounts data into output

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: Grouping parameter

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: List of fields for report (comma-separated). Available values: "min_call_duration", "avg_call_duration", "max_call_duration", "total_call_duration", "min_amount", "avg_amount", "max_amount", "total_amount", "first_time", "last_time", "count_incoming", "count_outgoing", "total"

parameters.description: By default grouping by date (week), will be based on GMT. You can adjust it by providing timezone offset in seconds (like "-18000" for New York)

parameters.name: filters[created_at]
parameters.description: Call log creation time. This filter is required and can be defined like this: filters[created_at]=between:1613999999,1614100000 (values are UNIX timestamps; not included into selection)

parameters.name: filters[direction]
parameters.description: Call direction (in seconds)

parameters.name: filters[extension]
parameters.description: Extension ID

parameters.name: filters[called_number]
parameters.description: Called number

parameters.name: filters[call_duration]
parameters.description: Call duration

parameters.name: filters[number]
parameters.description: Phone number (called number for inbound, caller id for outbound)

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Defines mode of the data output (brief or full)

post.description:
This API places a call to the caller phone number (or caller extension ID). If that number (or extension) answers within the timeout period, then the callee DID (or extension) will be connected to the call.

Make a phone call. You need to provide caller and callee information. You can provide different payload, so different parameters may be required. These are supported scenarios:
1. caller_phone_number + callee_extension.
2. caller_phone_number + callee_phone_number + caller_extension.
3. caller_extension + callee_phone_number.

3. caller_extension + callee_phone_number.

post.description:
Extension on your Phone.com account may have SIP devices associated with them. 

The Device API provides information about those SIP devices, such as the SIP credentials required.

The Create Device API allows you to add a Generic SIP device. You may use the extension API to associate the device with the extension, which is required for the device to be operational.  NOTE: If you use the Create Device and at the same request, ask to associate the device to an extension (using the "Lines" Param). The Call Handling Rules (CHR) of the Extension, if existed, will be deleted. 

To ensure proper operation of the SIP device, you must add some call handling rules to the extension. Adding CHR is usually done by using the extension API.

requestBody.description:

NOTE: If you use the Create Device and at the same request, ask to associate the device to an extension (using the "Lines" Param). The Call Handling Rules (CHR) of the Extension, if existed, will be deleted. 

To ensure proper operation of the SIP device, you must add some call handling rules to the extension. Adding CHR is usually done by using the extension API.

parameters.description: Contact name = first name + middle name + last name

parameters.name: filters[group_id]
parameters.description: Group filter

parameters.name: filters[phone]
parameters.description: Phone Number

parameters.name: filters[updated_at]
parameters.description: Filters by update date

parameters.name: filters[include_trashed]
parameters.description: only=deleted contacts; ignore=existing contacts; with=both deleted and existing contacts

parameters.name: filters[duplications]
parameters.description:
Use this filter to return contacts which have duplications. You can analyze them and resolve duplications the way you want on your side via other contacts endpoints. Possible values:
intersection - any match (name, email or number)
name - name is the same
email - email is the same
number - phone number is the same
number-or-email - email and/or number is the same

parameters.name: filters[deleted_at]
parameters.description: Filters by deletion date. Deleted records will be included automatically

parameters.name: filters[modified_at]
parameters.description: Filters by update or deletion date. Deleted records are not included by default.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Defines mode of the data output (brief or full)

[DEPRECATED; use Events Listeners] At the heart of every telephony system is the Events Engine. At Phone.com, our customers (and their call traffic) emit millions of events per day. These events play a key role in advanced integration opportunities such as CRM integration, security/auditing, and real-time contextual call control. The Events and Listeners API Services aim to simplify and standardize the method in which events are extracted from our systems and processed. These services provide consistent interfaces for internal systems to log events, manage subscriptions, and provide interfaces for external systems (e.g. customer applications or integrations such as Salesforce CRM) to subscribe to account-specific events and manage the subscriptions.

post.description:
Your Phone.com account has two kinds of media that can be used in various ways: Hold Music and Greetings. Hold Music can be used in routes, queues, and anywhere the caller might be placed on hold. Your account has access to a set of standard hold music options, and optionally another set of premium hold music options. You can also upload your own hold music, subject to copyright limitations. Greetings are verbal messages that can be played in various places to provide the caller with important information. For example, greetings are used when a caller first enters a menu or if they enter an invalid option. You also might use a greeting to alert the user to the fact that their call is being recorded.

Add a media object to your account that can be used as a greeting or hold music. Users may create a media by using the built-in Text-to-speech (TTS) facility or upload a file of their choice. (Note: The maximum size for media files or JSON objects included with a POST or PUT request is 10 MB)

Your Phone.com account has two kinds of media that can be used in various ways: Hold Music and Greetings. Hold Music can be used in routes, queues, and anywhere the caller might be placed on hold. Your account has access to a set of standard hold music options, and optionally another set of premium hold music options. You can also upload your own hold music, subject to copyright limitations. Greetings are verbal messages that can be played in various places to provide the caller with important information. For example, greetings are used when a caller first enters a menu or if they enter an invalid option. You also might use a greeting to alert the user to the fact that their call is being recorded.

post.description:
Phone.com’s standard phone service lets you send and receive SMS/MMS messages using your Phone.com numbers. The Phone.com API extends this functionality, letting you send SMS/MMS messages from your application (through your phone number and our API server) to your users. You can also use the API to retrieve the history of SMS/MMS sent from / delivered to your account.

This service is used to send a message.

The maximum message size (along with the attachments) is generally limited to 300KB (MMS 1.2), but recently the MMS 1.3 standard has allowed for a maximum size of 600KB. Wireless carriers, however, can impose their own size restrictions. Phone.com will try to send the file provided via the API as is; however, different vendors might reject the message according to their own policy.

This API endpoint is designated for Person to Person (P2P) communications. Improper use for marketing type purposes or Application to Person (A2P) will cause upstream cellular companies to block the “From” number for SMS use.  If you plan to use this endpoint for A2P or A2A applications that might also entail high volume, i.e., above the FREE limits set by the Phone.com plan, please contact our API support team at apisupport@phone.com to discuss other options.

The OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework enables a third-party application to obtain limited access to HTTP API requests which require an access token. This page describes how to use the /oauth/authorization and /oauth/access-token API's to generate an access token.

To create an access token via the /oauth/access-token API, an API user may choose any one of the grant types it supports: Authorization Code Grant, Client Credential Grant, Password Credential Grant or Refresh Token Grant. Provide "?mode=details" in query string to receive response identical to GET /v4/oauth/access-token/details

Queues are used when you want to direct calls to a pool of phone numbers and/or extensions. A common utilization of queues is for Customer Service call centers. When a queue is configured on your phone number, Phone.com will route incoming calls to members of the queue. If a queue member is already on another call, our service rings the next phone number or extension in the queue. With Queue Members endpoints you can manage queue members without updating the entire queue.

post.description:
Routes, a.k.a. 'Call Handling Rules' or 'Presets', are used to tell Phone.com how to handle incoming phone calls. They can be used to connect your Phone.com phone number to an extension, a menu, a calling queue, or many other things. The fundamental element of a route is an action. We support many different actions such as forward, menu, and voicemail. See below for the full details. In general, actions are processed one at a time in the order you specify, until the call is answered or disconnected. Answering or disconnecting stops any additional actions from being performed. Note that several actions such as  voicemail, menu, and fax completely take over processing the call and do not result in processing any subsequent actions. Actions are grouped into rule sets. Rule sets can be filtered based on the time of day or the caller's phone number. When processing an incoming call, Phone.com's routing engine will evaluate each rule set in the order that was specified when  the route was created or updated. The first matching rule set will be used, and all other rule sets will be ignored. Each route must have at least one unfiltered rule set. Note that if your route contains multiple unfiltered rule sets,  only the first one will be used.

Add a new route to the account

get.description:
[DEPRECATED; use Messages] Phone.com’s standard phone service lets you send and receive SMS messages using your Phone.com numbers. The Phone.com API extends this functionality, letting you send SMS messages from your application (through your phone number and our API server) to your users. You can also use the API to retrieve the history of sms sent from / delivered to your account.

Get a list of SMS messages for an account.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of items to skip before starting to collect the result set

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return

parameters.name: sort[id]
parameters.description: Sort response

parameters.name: filters[id]
parameters.description: ID filter

parameters.name: filters[is_new]
parameters.description: 1 when SMS is new; 0 when SMS has been read.

parameters.name: filters[from]
parameters.description: Caller ID filter, the E.164 phone number to send the SMS FROM. Note: you must encode the + as %2B, and the direction filter is required.

parameters.name: filters[to]
parameters.description: Callee ID filter, the E.164 phone number to send the SMS TO. Note you must encode the + as %2B, and the direction filter is required.

parameters.name: filters[direction]
parameters.description: Direction filter, in or out

parameters.name: filters[created_at]
parameters.description: Date string representing the UTC time that sms was created. Expected value is UNIX timestamp with operator like "gt" (greater than), "gte" (greater than or equals), "lt" (lesser than), "lte" (lesser than or equals). Example: "gt:1597778180".

parameters.name: filters[updated_at]
parameters.description: Date string representing the UTC time that sms was updated. Expected value is UNIX timestamp with operator like "gt" (greater than), "gte" (greater than or equals), "lt" (lesser than), "lte" (lesser than or equals). Example: "gt:1597778180".

parameters.name: filters[extension]
parameters.description: Extension id where SMS is stored

parameters.name: filters[deleted]
parameters.description: only=deleted SMS; ignore=existing SMS; with=both deleted and existing SMS

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: Defines mode of the data output (brief or full)

Normally each video-user is billed to the account and the pay is based on the plan selected.

Create a user video for extension. The user will receive an email with steps to connect to their video bridge and additional operational instructions.

tags.name: Asynchronous Call Logs
tags.description:
Phone.com created the async-call-logs API endpoint to allow customers to download large amounts of call logs without encountering the normal Phone.com API Rate Limits. 
This service needs to be activated on a per-customer basis and collect the call log data in the requested customer's format. (please call our support to activate and better define the format if needed) 
When the async-call-logs endpoint is invoked, the endpoint will return a list of 47 to 48 files, each representing the calls entered into the logs during a full hour of the last two days. The file names contain the time period of call logs each file encapsulates. 
The Items returned are ordered by modification (i.e., creation) date; the most recent item is the first item. The files should be download using the provided URL(s) in the item JSON array returned by the endpoint. 
Phone.com creates the files at the 15 minutes mark, every hour for the previous hour. Files lifespan is 2 days, which means that each file will disappear after 48 hours.

tags.description: This service lists phone numbers that are available for purchase through Phone.com.

tags.name: Blocked Calls
tags.description: The Blocked Calls API allows users block incoming calls from specific phone numbers. If only a part of a telephone number is provided, calls with callers ID matching the pattern will be blocked. For example, pattern '+1212' will block all calls from NY 212 area code.

tags.name: Call Logs
tags.description: The Call Logs API returns a list of call detail records for the account. The result includes the id, start time, creation time, caller ID, called number, direction of call, and duration in seconds. If applicable, the result may also include final action, final action duration, listen URL, and/or transcription. Since the call logs usually contains many calls, API users may use filters such as start_time, created_at and direction to limit the result set to calls interested. Users may also use the sorting parameters such as start_time and created_at to list result in ascending or descending order.

tags.name: Listeners
tags.description: [DEPRECATED; use Events Listeners] At the heart of every telephony system is the Events Engine. At Phone.com, our customers (and their call traffic) emit millions of events per day. These events play a key role in advanced integration opportunities such as CRM integration, security/auditing, and real-time contextual call control. The Events and Listeners API Services aim to simplify and standardize the method in which events are extracted from our systems and processed. These services provide consistent interfaces for internal systems to log events, manage subscriptions, and provide interfaces for external systems (e.g. customer applications or integrations such as Salesforce CRM) to subscribe to account-specific events and manage the subscriptions.

tags.name: Media
tags.description: Your Phone.com account has two kinds of media that can be used in various ways: Hold Music and Greetings. Hold Music can be used in routes, queues, and anywhere the caller might be placed on hold. Your account has access to a set of standard hold music options, and optionally another set of premium hold music options. You can also upload your own hold music, subject to copyright limitations. Greetings are verbal messages that can be played in various places to provide the caller with important information. For example, greetings are used when a caller first enters a menu or if they enter an invalid option. You also might use a greeting to alert the user to the fact that their call is being recorded.

tags.name: Queue Members
tags.description: Queues are used when you want to direct calls to a pool of phone numbers and/or extensions. A common utilization of queues is for Customer Service call centers. When a queue is configured on your phone number, Phone.com will route incoming calls to members of the queue. If a queue member is already on another call, our service rings the next phone number or extension in the queue. With Queue Members endpoints you can manage queue members without updating the entire queue.

When a queue is configured on your phone number, Phone.com will route incoming calls to members of the queue. If a queue member is already on another call, our service rings the next phone number or extension in the queue. While your callers are waiting to be connected, you can choose to play Hold Music.

tags.name: Routes
tags.description: Routes, a.k.a. 'Call Handling Rules' or 'Presets', are used to tell Phone.com how to handle incoming phone calls. They can be used to connect your Phone.com phone number to an extension, a menu, a calling queue, or many other things. The fundamental element of a route is an action. We support many different actions such as forward, menu, and voicemail. See below for the full details. In general, actions are processed one at a time in the order you specify, until the call is answered or disconnected. Answering or disconnecting stops any additional actions from being performed. Note that several actions such as  voicemail, menu, and fax completely take over processing the call and do not result in processing any subsequent actions. Actions are grouped into rule sets. Rule sets can be filtered based on the time of day or the caller's phone number. When processing an incoming call, Phone.com's routing engine will evaluate each rule set in the order that was specified when  the route was created or updated. The first matching rule set will be used, and all other rule sets will be ignored. Each route must have at least one unfiltered rule set. Note that if your route contains multiple unfiltered rule sets,  only the first one will be used.
